test.name: Search request should not trigger record-extraction
test.description: User asks to FIND a record. record-extraction analyzes records already in context; finding records is search-records. Boundary that the description optimizer cares about.

input.user_message: Find the 1860 census record for Patrick Flynn in Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania.

negative.explanation: The user wants to search for a record they haven't found yet, not analyze a record already in context. 'Find' is the trigger word for search-records. There is no record data in Claude's context to extract from — record-extraction would have nothing to do.

judge_context: Should route to search-records explicitly; should not invent record content to extract from
